cards.front: Where does IPv6 appear explicitly in the CCNA 200-301 v1.1 blueprint?
cards.back: Three places: 1.8 (Configure + verify IPv6 addressing/prefix), 1.9 (Describe IPv6 address types), 3.3 (Configure + verify IPv4 and IPv6 static routing).

cards.front: Which generic-IP blueprint topics implicitly include IPv6?
cards.back: 1.10 (Verify IP parameters for Client OS), 3.1 (routing-table components), 3.2 (forwarding decision), 3.5 (FHRP — HSRPv2/VRRPv3/GLBP support v6).

cards.front: RFC 5952 leading-zero rule
cards.back: Drop leading zeros within each hextet (`0042` → `42`, `0000` → `0`).

cards.front: Drop leading zeros within each hextet (`0042` → `42`, `0000` → `0`).
RFC 5952 double-colon rule
cards.back: Replace the **longest** run of consecutive all-zero hextets with `::`. Use `::` **only once** per address.

cards.front: Replace the **longest** run of consecutive all-zero hextets with `::`. Use `::` **only once** per address.
If two zero-runs of equal length exist in an IPv6 address, which gets compressed?
Standard IPv6 prefix length for a point-to-point link (RFC 6164)

cards.back: Standard IPv6 prefix length for a point-to-point link (RFC 6164)

cards.front: Prefix length for a single IPv6 host

cards.back: Prefix length for a single IPv6 host

cards.front: First three bits of any global unicast address

cards.back: First three bits of any global unicast address

cards.front: Global command required for a router to forward IPv6 packets
`ipv6 unicast-routing`
cards.back: Global command required for a router to forward IPv6 packets

cards.front: If `ipv6 unicast-routing` is NOT configured, what happens?
cards.back: Router holds IPv6 addresses on interfaces but will not forward IPv6 packets between them.

cards.front: Manual full IPv6 interface address command (no auto-gen)
`ipv6 address 2001:db8:1::1/64`
cards.back: Manual full IPv6 interface address command (no auto-gen)

cards.front: Interface command to set the network prefix and auto-generate the interface ID via EUI-64
`ipv6 address 2001:db8:1::/64 eui-64`
cards.back: Interface command to set the network prefix and auto-generate the interface ID via EUI-64

cards.front: Interface command that gives the interface a link-local address only, no global
`ipv6 enable`
cards.back: Interface command that gives the interface a link-local address only, no global

cards.front: When is a link-local address auto-generated on an interface?
cards.back: As soon as IPv6 is enabled on the interface (via any IPv6 address command or `ipv6 enable`). FE80::/10 + EUI-64 (or random).

cards.front: Show all IPv6 addresses per interface in one line each
`show ipv6 interface brief`
cards.back: Show all IPv6 addresses per interface in one line each

cards.front: Show detailed IPv6 info for one interface — link-local, global, joined multicast groups, MTU, ND timers
`show ipv6 interface <intf>`
cards.back: Show detailed IPv6 info for one interface — link-local, global, joined multicast groups, MTU, ND timers

cards.front: Display the IPv6 routing table
`show ipv6 route`
IPv6 equivalent of the IPv4 ARP cache
`show ipv6 neighbors` (NDP cache)
cards.back: IPv6 equivalent of the IPv4 ARP cache

cards.front: To ping a link-local address you must also specify the [...] because link-local is not globally unique.
